text.title: Azure NSG Rules and Flow Logs Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Azure NSG Rules and Flow Logs` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every NSG MUST include a named `DenyAllInbound` rule at priority 4096 with source/destination/protocol/port = *. Implicit default deny is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit default deny is invisible in audit logs + Terraform plans; explicit deny is testable.

rule.statement: Allow rules MUST specify exact source CIDR / service tag and exact destination ports. Wildcard source + destination on allow is forbidden (except `AzureLoadBalancer` health probe).
rule.rationale: `Allow 80/443 from 0.0.0.0/0` is the #1 misconfiguration; exact CIDR caps blast radius.

rule.statement: NSG flow logs MUST be enabled at version 2 with Traffic Analytics on every production NSG. Retention MUST be ≥ 30 days.

rationale.source: Azure flow logs docs + PCI logging requirements

rule.rationale: Without flow logs, east-west incidents are undiagnosable; v2 adds byte/packet counts.

rule.statement: Network Watcher MUST be enabled in every region that has production NSGs.
rule.rationale: Flow logs require Network Watcher; missing means flow logs silently disabled.

rule.statement: NSG rules MUST be audited quarterly; rules with no recent hits + no owner annotation are removed.
rule.rationale: Unused legacy allow rules accumulate; quarterly audit prunes them.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
